# UC-19.1.47 · Dell VxRail Physical Disk Predictive Failure and Unhealthy Drive Status

## Description

VxRail nodes depend on local drives for cache and capacity tiers before vSAN rebuilds complete. Predictive failure and offline disk states are leading indicators of rebuild traffic, reduced fault tolerance, and potential double-fault risk.

## Value

Lets operators open Dell support cases and schedule drive swaps during business hours instead of during a vSAN resync emergency.

## Implementation

Extend your existing VxRail modular input to poll physical disk inventory per node. Map vendor fields to `predictive_failure`, `smart_alert`, and `disk_state`. Supplement with iDRAC SEL forwarded as syslog for cross-check. Alert on first predictive flag per serial.

## SPL

```spl
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:physical_disk" earliest=-24h
| where predictive_failure=1 OR lower(disk_state) IN ("failed", "offline", "unknown") OR smart_alert=1
| stats values(disk_state) as state, values(predictive_failure) as pred by host, serial_number, slot
| sort host, slot
```

## Visualization

Table (at-risk disks by node), Single value (predictive count), Timeline (disk state changes).

## References

- [Dell VxRail — Hardware replacement overview](https://www.dell.com/support/manuals/en-us/vxrail-appliance-series-docs/vxrail-8.0.x-maintenance-guide)
